info.title: Deno Deploy API
info.description: Download OpenAPI Spec API for managing apps, revisions, layers, and runtime logs on Deno Deploy. ## Conventions - All field names use `snake_case` - Cursor-based pagination with `cursor` and `limit` parameters - Streaming endpoints support both JSONL (`application/x-ndjson`) and SSE (`text/event-stream`). Use the `Accept` header to specify the desired format. ## Environment Variables Environment variables can be set on layers, apps, and revisions. They support **context filtering**, allowing different values for different deployment contexts (e.g., `production`, `preview`, `build`). Resolution order (later overrides earlier): 1. Organization-wide env vars 2. App's layer env vars (flattened, in `layers` array order) 3. App-specific env vars 4. Revision's layer env vars (flattened, in `layers` array order) 5. Revision-specific env vars Within each layer level (2 and 4), later entries in the `layers` array override earlier ones. When a layer itself includes base layers, those bases are resolved first (depth-first), then the including layer's own env vars take precedence. For example, if `app.layers` is `[X]` and layer X includes `[A, B]`, the resolution within level 2 is: A → B → X (X's own vars win). When updating via PATCH, `env_vars` performs a **deep merge**: update by `id`, update by `key`+`contexts` match, or create new. Set `delete: true` to remove. ## Pagination All list endpoints use cursor-based pagination. When more results exist, the response includes a `Link` header: ``` Link: ; rel="next" ``` ## Error Responses All errors follow this format: ```json {"error": {"code": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human-readable message"}} ```

tags.name: apps
tags.description:
An app is the top-level container for a deployable application.
Apps have configuration (build settings, environment variables), can reference layers for shared config, and contain revisions (deployments).

**Key characteristics:**

- Identified by UUID or human-readable slug (slugs cannot contain underscores, IDs always do)
- Support up to 5 labels for filtering and grouping
- Reference layers via `layers` array for inherited configuration
- Have app-specific `env_vars` that override layer values
- Have a `config` that provides defaults for revisions

tags.name: revisions
tags.description:
A revision represents a specific build and deployment of an app. Revisions are immutable once created — to make changes, you create a new revision.

Status lifecycle: `queued` → `building` → `succeeded` (success), `queued` → `failed` (build error, cancelled, or timeout), or `queued` → `skipped`.

tags.name: layers
tags.description:
A layer is a mutable configuration object that can be shared across multiple apps. Layers provide the solution for bulk environment variable management: instead of updating thousands of apps individually, you create a layer, attach it to apps, then update the layer once.

Key characteristics:
- Organization-scoped and identified by ID or slug
- Contain environment variables
- Layers can include other layers (base layers), enabling hierarchical configuration. Base layers are resolved depth-first: base env vars are applied in array order (later overrides earlier), then the including layer's own env vars take precedence over all its bases
- Apps reference layers in their `layers` array
- Updating a layer is O(1) regardless of how many apps reference it
- Layer updates cause running isolates to restart but do not require redeployment

id.description: Unique identifier for the environment variable

value.description: The value. Omitted when `secret` is true

secret.description: Whether the value is masked in API responses

contexts.description: Deployment contexts this variable applies to. `"all"` means every context.

secret.description: Whether to mask the value in API responses. Defaults to false

contexts.description: Deployment contexts this variable applies to. Defaults to `"all"`.

id.description: ID of the existing variable to update or delete

key.description: Variable name. Used for matching when `id` is not provided

secret.description: Whether to mask the value in API responses

contexts.description: Deployment contexts this variable applies to

delete.description: Set to true to remove this variable

type.description: `dynamic` runs a Deno process; `static` serves pre-built files

entrypoint.description: Main module path. Required when `type` is `dynamic`

args.description: Additional CLI arguments passed to the entrypoint

cwd.description: Working directory or static file root. Required when `type` is `static`

spa.description: Enable single-page application mode (fallback to index.html). Only for `static` type

install.description: Custom install command. Omit to skip the install step

build.description: Custom build command. Omit to skip the build step

predeploy.description: Command to run before each deployment (e.g. database migrations). Omit to skip

framework.description: Framework preset used for this build

target.description: Relative path to the symlink target

get.description: Get detailed information about an app, including labels, layers, environment variables, and config.

patch.description:
All fields are optional. `labels` and `layers` replace the entire value. `env_vars` performs a deep merge with existing variables. `config` replaces the entire deploy config (no deep merge).

Updating `layers` or `env_vars` will restart running isolates.

env_vars.description: Deep merge with existing environment variables

delete.description: Delete an app and all its revisions.

get.description:
List apps with optional filtering by labels or layer.

Use `labels[key]=value` query parameters to filter by label values. Use `layer` to filter apps that reference a specific layer.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Layer ID or slug. Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

post.description: Apps can reference layers for shared configuration, have app-specific environment variables, and a config that provides defaults for revisions.

slug.description: App slug. If omitted, a random slug is generated

labels.description: Key-value labels for filtering and grouping (max 5)

layers.description: Layers to reference for inherited configuration

post.description:
Create a new revision (deployment).

Upload source files as assets and optionally specify `config`, `layers`, `env_vars`, and `labels`. Asset keys are relative paths resolved against `/app/src`.

If `config` is omitted, it is inherited from the app's config. If specified, it fully replaces the app's config (no deep merge).

Revision `env_vars` are immutable once created and have highest priority in the resolution order. Context filtering is not supported for revision env vars.

Use `production` and `preview` to control which timelines the revision is deployed to. By default, revisions are deployed to the production timeline only.

assets.description: Source files to deploy. Keys are paths relative to `/app/src`

config.description: Build and runtime config. If omitted, inherited from the app

layers.description: Layers to reference for this revision

labels.description: Metadata labels (e.g. git branch, commit SHA)

production.description: Whether to deploy to the production timeline. Defaults to true

preview.description: Whether to deploy as a preview deployment. Defaults to false

get.description:
Query historical runtime logs.

Use `stream=true` to stream runtime logs in real-time using Server-Sent Events or JSONL.

When streaming, event types include: `log` (a log entry), `heartbeat` (keep-alive sent every 30 seconds), and `error` (stream error).

parameters.name: end

schema.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601). Defaults to now

parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601). Defaults to now

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: stream

schema.description: Set to true to stream logs in real-time via SSE or JSONL

parameters.description: Set to true to stream logs in real-time via SSE or JSONL

get.description:
Get revision details.

Revision IDs are globally unique. The response includes `layers`, `env_vars`, and `config` when available.

Status lifecycle (one of):
- queued -> building -> succeeded (success)
- queued -> failed (build error, cancelled, or timeout)
- queued -> skipped (e.g., commit message contains [skip-ci])

post.description: Request cancellation of a build in progress. Cancellation is asynchronous — this endpoint returns immediately with the current revision state. The `cancellation_requested_at` field will be set, but the revision may still be in `building` status. Poll the revision or use the [/progress](#tag/revisions/GET/api/v2/revisions/{revision}/progress) endpoint to wait for the build to reach the `failed` state with `failure_reason: "cancelled"`.

get.description: List revisions for an app. Optionally filter by status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Stream revision build progress. The stream ends when the revision
reaches a terminal state (`succeeded`, `failed`, or `skipped`).

Supports both JSONL (`Accept: application/x-ndjson`) and SSE (`Accept: text/event-stream`)
formats via the `Accept` header.

Stream build logs for a revision.

Supports both Server-Sent Events (SSE) (`Accept: text/event-stream`) and JSON Lines (`Accept: application/x-ndjson`) formats. Use the `Accept` header to specify the desired format.

The stream remains open during active builds and closes when the build completes.
Get the timelines (deployment targets) where this revision is active.

description.description: Optional description of the layer's purpose

layers.description: Other layers to include for hierarchical configuration

get.description: List all layers in the organization.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Get a layer by ID or slug.

Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Layer ID or slug. Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

patch.description:
Update a layer. This is the key operation for bulk environment variable updates.

All fields are optional. `env_vars` performs a deep merge with existing variables: update by ID, update by key+contexts match, or create new. Set `delete: true` to remove a variable.

Running isolates will restart to pick up the new configuration.

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Layer ID or slug. Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

env_vars.description: Deep merge with existing environment variables

delete.description: Returns 409 Conflict if apps still reference this layer.

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Layer ID or slug. Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

get.description:
List apps that reference this layer.

The `layer_position` indicates the index in each app's `layers` array.

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Layer ID or slug. Slugs cannot contain underscores; IDs always do.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return
